management_notes: Management claims 20+ years of West African gold exploration experience with multiple discoveries later acquired by majors including IAMGOLD. Director Jean-Marc Gagnon has 35+ years in mining including West African project development. Director Paul Ténière (M.Sc., P.Geo.) is QP. However, company is micro-cap (~C$3-5M market cap) and Guinea presents significant political and operational risk. IAMGOLD's adjacent Karita discovery validates the geological neighborhood but not Alma's own ground.
geology_notes: Flagship Karita West Project is 392.1 km² in Guinea, immediately adjacent to IAMGOLD's Karita permit (where IAMGOLD drilled 34m @ 5.81 g/t Au). Geological setting (Birimian-aged rocks, near Senegal-Mali Shear Zone) is highly prospective for gold. Alma has no NI 43-101 resource — only early-stage sampling (termite mound programs, planned air core drilling). Clarence Stream North in New Brunswick also held. All projects are grass-roots.
capital_notes: Share consolidation on 10:1 basis resulted in approximately 6,774,562 post-consolidated shares initially; CSE listing shows 57,745,285 shares issued and outstanding (reflects post-consolidation and subsequent issuances). Market cap approximately C$3-8M. No significant warrant or option data confirmed publicly. Micro-cap structure limits financing options.
catalyst_notes: No drilling program announced as imminent. Company completing preliminary work (termite mound sampling, aeromagnetics) at Karita West in preparation for a maiden drill program. No resource estimate timeline disclosed. Guinea political environment (military government since 2021 coup) adds permitting uncertainty. Nearest material catalyst is a maiden drill program announcement and results — timeline uncertain.
acquisition_notes: No resource estimate means no NAV or P/NAV calculation possible. Adjacency to IAMGOLD's Karita deposit provides geological optionality but no monetizable value until drilling proves up a resource. Guinea jurisdiction risk (military junta, nationalization history) significantly discounts any acquisition premium. Very high-risk, no comparables applicable.
analyst_summary: Alma Gold is a micro-cap (C$3-8M) early-stage explorer in Guinea with zero NI 43-101 resources and a dependency on proximity to IAMGOLD's nearby Karita gold discovery as its primary geological thesis. While the Birimian gold belt of West Africa is genuinely prospective and management has relevant regional experience, Guinea's political risk (military government since 2021) and the complete absence of a drill-defined resource make this uninvestable for most retail investors at this stage. Revisit only after maiden drilling confirms continuity with IAMGOLD's mineralization.
